classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: “I saw another mighty angel coming down from heaven, clothed with a cloud; and a rainbow was upon his head, and his face was as it were the sun, and his feet as pillars of fire: and he had in his hand an open book: and he set his right foot upon the sea, and his left foot upon the earth.”]

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: He signifies that that mighty angel who, he says, descended from heaven, clothed with a cloud, is our Lord, as we have above narrated.

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: “His face was as it were the sun.”]

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: That is, with respect to the resurrection.

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: “Upon his head was a rainbow.”]

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: He points to the judgment which is executed by Him, or shall be.

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: “An open book.”]

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: A revelation of works in the future judgment, or the Apocalypse which John received.

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: “His feet,”] as we have said above, are the apostles.

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: For that both things in sea and land are trodden under foot by Him, signifies that all things are placed under His feet.

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: Moreover, he calls Him an angel, that is, a messenger, to wit, of the Father; for He is called the Messenger of great counsel.

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: He says also that He cried with a loud voice.

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: The great voice is to tell the words of the Omnipotent God of heaven to men, and to bear witness that after penitence is closed there will be no hope subsequently.

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: “Seven thunders uttered their voices.”]

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: The seven thunders uttering their voices signify the Holy Spirit of sevenfold power, who through the prophets announced all things to come, and by His voice John gave his testimony in the world; but because he says that he was about to write the things which the thunders had uttered, that is, whatever things had been obscure in the announcements of the Old Testament; he is forbidden to write them, but he was charged to leave them sealed, because he is an apostle, nor was it fitting that the grace of the subsequent stage should be given in the first.

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: “The time,” says he, “is at hand.”

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: For the apostles, by powers, by signs, by portents, and by mighty works, have overcome unbelief.

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: After them there is now given to the same completed Churches the comfort of having the prophetic Scriptures subsequently interpreted, for I said that after the apostles there would be interpreting prophets.

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: For the apostle says: “And he placed in the Church indeed, first, apostles; secondly, prophets; thirdly, teachers,” and the rest.

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: And in another place he says: “Let the prophets speak two or three, and let the others judge.”

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: And he says: “Every woman that prayeth or prophesieth with her head uncovered, dishonoureth her head.”

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: And when he says, “Let the prophets speak two or three, and let the others judge,” he is not speaking in respect of the Catholic prophecy of things unheard and unknown, but of things both announced and known.

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: But let them judge whether or not the interpretation is consistent with the testimonies of the prophetic utterance.

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: It is plain, therefore, that to John, armed as he was with superior virtue, this was not necessary, although the body of Christ, which is the Church, adorned with His members, ought to respond to its position.

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: “I took the book from the hand of the angel, and ate it up.”]

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: To take the book and eat it up, is, when exhibition of a thing is made to one, to commit it to memory.

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: “And it was in my mouth as sweet as honey.”]

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: To be sweet in the mouth is the reward of the preaching of the speaker, and is most pleasant to the hearers; but it is most bitter both to those that announce it, and to those that persevere in its commandments through suffering.

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: “And He says unto me, Thou must again prophesy to the peoples, and to the tongues, and to the nations, and to many kings.”]

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: He says this, because when John said these things he was in the island of Patmos, condemned to the labour of the mines by Cæsar Domitian.

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: There, therefore, he saw the Apocalypse; and when grown old, he thought that he should at length receive his quittance by suffering, Domitian being killed, all his judgments were discharged.

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: And John being dismissed from the mines, thus subsequently delivered the same Apocalypse which he had received from God.

verses.book: Commentary on the Apocalypse of the Blessed John
verses.text: This, therefore, is what He says: Thou must again prophesy to all nations, because thou seest the crowds of Antichrist rise up; and against them other crowds shall stand, and they shall fall by the sword on the one side and on the other.
